text.title: csharp-dotnet-patterns Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for csharp-dotnet-patterns as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Agent adds behavior to Application handler instead of Domain entity method.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Business logic in handlers breaks the DDD invariant; entities become property bags and the architecture's value evaporates. Prompt agents to read Domain/Entities/ first.

failure-mode.description: Agent imports Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore into the Application project.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: NetArchTest catches this; without it, agents drift within weeks and the layering benefit is lost.

failure-mode.description: Domain events raised at SaveChanges inside the same transaction as handlers that also write to DB.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Handler exceptions roll back the outer transaction unexpectedly. Use the outbox pattern (MassTransit) for cross-aggregate events.

failure-mode.description: MediatR overused: GET /health, simple lookups wrapped in Commands/Queries.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Every MediatR dispatch pays handler resolution + pipeline behavior overhead. Reserve for use cases with cross-cutting needs (validation, auth, transactions).

failure-mode.description: Entity collection mutated outside aggregate: handler does user.Posts.Add(post) directly.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Encapsulate collection mutation in aggregate methods (user.AddPost(post)); outside mutation bypasses invariants and domain events.

failure-mode.description: IgnoreQueryFilters() used outside explicit admin tools.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Multi-tenant global query filter bypassed silently; data from other tenants leaks. Lint to forbid this call outside designated admin repositories.

failure-mode.description: Anemic regression. Agent adds new behavior to UserService (Application) instead of User entity (Domain). Force prompts to read Domain/Entities/<Aggregate>.cs first; require behavior changes there.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Business logic in handlers breaks DDD invariant; entities become property bags.

failure-mode.description: Layer leakage. Agent imports Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore into Application for "convenience". NetArchTest catches; without it, agents drift over time.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Couples presentation layer to persistence and defeats the layering purpose.

failure-mode.description: Domain events vs Integration events confusion. Agents fire a domain event for cross-service notification; these don't cross process boundaries. Document which is which in Domain/Events/ vs Application/IntegrationEvents/.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Domain events are intra-aggregate; integration events span services and need outbox pattern.

failure-mode.description: DbContext.SaveChangesAsync ordering. Domain event handlers running inside the same transaction can deadlock or roll back unexpectedly. Use outbox pattern (MassTransit) for anything crossing aggregates.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Handler exceptions roll back the outer transaction unexpectedly.

failure-mode.description: Validator duplication. Agent adds RuleFor(x => x.Email).EmailAddress() in three places (entity, validator, DTO annotation). Standardize: validators on Commands, invariants in entity factories, no annotations.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Duplication leads to inconsistency and maintenance burden.

failure-mode.description: AutoMapper runtime errors. Agent adds a property without mapping; tests pass, prod 500s. Add MapperConfiguration.AssertConfigurationIsValid() as a unit test.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Source-generated mapping catches misses at compile time; reflection does not.

failure-mode.description: Handler returning Task. Agent forgets Task<TResponse>; compile error or wrong return shape. Roslynator rule MA0042 catches.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Wrong return type breaks the CQRS contract.

failure-mode.description: async void in event handlers. Agent uses async void Handle(...) for "fire-and-forget"; one exception kills the host. INotificationHandler<T> must return Task.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: async void suppresses exceptions and crashes the application.

failure-mode.description: MediatR over-use for trivial paths. Agent wraps GET /health in a Query/Handler. Reserve MediatR for use cases with cross-cutting needs.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Every MediatR dispatch pays handler resolution + pipeline behavior overhead.

failure-mode.description: Entity collection mutation outside aggregate. Agent does user.Posts.Add(post) from a handler. Add via user.AddPost(post) — encapsulate.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Outside mutation bypasses invariants and domain events.

failure-mode.description: Soft-delete + global query filter forgotten on a query. Agent uses IgnoreQueryFilters() to "see all rows" and ships it; multi-tenant leak. Lint forbids IgnoreQueryFilters outside admin tools.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Multi-tenant global query filter bypassed silently; data from other tenants leaks.

failure-mode.description: Migration generated by agent but not applied to seed/integration tests. PR builds fail intermittently; agents blame flaky tests. Force dotnet ef database update on test container startup.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Test isolation requires fresh schema on each test run.

failure-mode.description: MediatR/AutoMapper license accepted by accident. Agent adds via dotnet add package; commercial license trip wires fire later. Pin SPDX checks in CI.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: License changes may impose commercial obligations.
